plan.action: Diagnose the failing test: test #22 removes '${WORKSPACE_ROOT}/Agents/tech.md' but tech_discover checks '${CLIPPY_DIR}/Agents/tech.md' (the real repo dir), so the removal has no effect. The fix is to make agents_dir configurable or to fall through to the workspace-relative path.

Add an optional CLIPPY_AGENTS_DIR env override (defaulting to '${CLIPPY_DIR}/Agents') to tech_discover so tests can redirect the agents dir to the temporary workspace. Update tech_discover to use: agents_dir="${CLIPPY_AGENTS_DIR:-${CLIPPY_DIR}/Agents}".

Update the test setup in tests/tech.bats to export CLIPPY_AGENTS_DIR="${AGENTS_DIR}" (which already points to the tmp workspace Agents dir). This lets the existing stub removal in test #22 actually affect the directory the code checks.
Run bats tests/tech.bats to confirm all 23 tests pass, including the previously failing test #22.
Verify the full test suite (bats tests/) still passes with no regressions.

testing_recommendations: Run bats tests/tech.bats in isolation first — all 23 tests must pass including test #22 'tech_discover skips gracefully when tech agent file is missing'
Run the full bats test suite (bats tests/) to check for regressions — particularly loop.bats and agents.bats which depend on adjacent modules
Verify test #15 'tech_discover skips when tech.md already has content' still passes — it must not be broken by the env var change
Confirm tech_discover still uses CLIPPY_DIR/Agents by default when CLIPPY_AGENTS_DIR is unset (default path must be preserved)
risks: Other tests may export or rely on CLIPPY_AGENTS_DIR already — check all .bats files for that variable name before adding
If tech_run_agent also hardcodes the agents_dir reference it may need the same override treatment — review carefully
